content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration · · · Director, Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5860        322 5901        322 6461        322 6444

April 6, 1978


Jack E. Frankel
Executive Officer
Commission on Judicial
Performance
3018 State Building
San Francisco, CA 94102

Dear Mr. Frankel:

     The Fair Political Practices Commission has received your
letter dated March 28, 1978, requesting an opinion or letter of ad-
vice on whether the Commission on Judicial Performance ("Commission")
is covered by the provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").
Since your letter does not present a substantial question of inter-
pretation, we are responding by a letter of advice rather than a
formal opinion.

     With the exception of the disclosure requirements imposed
on judges of courts of record by Government Code Section 872001/, the
provisions of the Act do not apply to "the courts or any agency in
the judicial branch of government." Section 82049.

     From a review of the constitutional provisions establish-
ing the Commission, Article VI, Sections 8 and 18, as well as a
cursory reading of several cases involving the Commission, we con-
clude that the Commission is an agency in the judicial branch of
government. Consequently, it is not covered by the Act. Specifi-
cally, the Commission is not an "agency" within the meaning of the
Act. Sections 82003 and 82049. Accordingly, the Commission is not
required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. Section 87300. Simi-
larly, the members and employees are not "public officials," Section
82048, and are not subject to the general disqualification provisions
set forth in Sections 87100, et seq. Of course, members of the Com-
mission who are judges of courts of record are required under


1/ All further statutory references are to the Government
/Code.

Jack E. Frankel
Page Two
April 6, 1978


Section 87200§/ to file Statements of Economic Interests disclosing
income, interests in real property and investments.

This letter constitutes written advice pursuant to 2 Cal.
Aim. Code Section 18329. If you have any questions, please feel
free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Barbara Campbell
Counsel
Legal Division

BC:jo


2/ When the Act was approved initially in 1974, there
was no requirement in Section 87200 that judges file Statements
of Economic Interests. That requirement was added by the Legislature
in 1976. (Stats. 1976, Ch. 129.)

JUSTIZBEREICH D.JAMES

JUSTIZBEREICH
GEMEINDE SPEYER

Speyer's Gemeinderat

Gemeinderat Rheinardt Plattenbund

Unterzeichnet aus Gründen des

Speyer Gemeindegerichts

March 23, 1978

Pfalz Politisches Commission

P.O. Box 807 Catharonia 95814

Gentlemen:

The Commission Judicial Procedure requests an
opinion or letter to whether the Complaint
is covered by the Political Crimes Section 5 and 18 Forl list
Commission is essential we are a party is attached.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]

JABER: (illegible)
Enclosure

sections.question: whether the Commission on Judicial Performance ("Commission") is covered by the provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act")
sections.conclusion: From a review of the constitutional provisions establishing the Commission, Article VI, Sections 8 and 18, as well as a cursory reading of several cases involving the Commission, we conclude that the Commission is an agency in the judicial branch of government. Consequently, it is not covered by the Act. Specifically the Commission is not an "agency" within the meaning of the Act. Sections 82003 and 82049. Accordingly, the Commission is not required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. Section 87300. Similarly, the members and employees are not "public officials," Section 82048, and are not subject to the general disqualification provisions set forth in Sections 87100, et seq.
sections.question_synthetic: The Commission on Judicial Performance sought clarification on whether it is subject to the Political Reform Act's requirements, including disclosure requirements and conflict of interest code provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the Commission on Judicial Performance, as an agency in the judicial branch of government, is not covered by the Political Reform Act and therefore is not required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code or subject its members to general disqualification provisions. However, judges who are members and required to file under Section 87200 must still file Statements of Economic Interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated March 28, 1978) and FPPC response letter (dated April 6, 1978). Extracted primary response from FPPC Counsel Barbara Campbell. Clear question and conclusion sections present. Statutory references provided throughout.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether the Commission on Judicial Performance ("Commission") is covered by the provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act")

CONCLUSION: From a review of the constitutional provisions establishing the Commission, Article VI, Sections 8 and 18, as well as a cursory reading of several cases involving the Commission, we conclude that the Commission is an agency in the judicial branch of government. Consequently, it is not covered by the Act. Specifically the Commission is not an "agency" within the meaning of the Act. Sections 82003 and 82049. Accordingly, the Commission is not required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. Section 87300. Similarly, the members and employees are not "public officials," Section 82048, and are not subject to the general disqualification provisions set forth in Sections 87100, et seq.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised the Commission on Judicial Performance that it is not covered by the Political Reform Act because it is an agency in the judicial branch of government, with the exception that judges serving on the Commission must file Statements of Economic Interests.
